# Traceability & Accountability

**Flexibility: Required (MUST)**

## The Commitment

Every action in the system — human or automated — has an identifiable owner
and a recoverable audit trail. No action is anonymous. No decision is untraceable.
The question "who did this, when, and why?" must always have an answer.

## What This Means

- Every action produces a structured log entry with actor, timestamp, and context.
- Ephemeral work (flows, scripts, one-shot operations) is logged even though the executor is temporary.
- Decision provenance is traceable: from decision back to the discussion, data, and actors that produced it.
- Audit trails are append-only. Historical records are never modified.

## Violation Looks Like

A configuration change with no record of who changed it or why. An automated
process that runs without logging. A decision that exists but nobody can trace
how it was made.

## Derived From

- JOS-C06 (Documentation Discipline)
- JOS-C05 (Data Primacy)

### Framework Connection

Framework §6 (ownership meta-component) ensures every action has a routable owner, while §14 (revision identity) gives every change a structured trace record linking actor, time, and reason — making traceability a structural property, not just a practice.
